text: One of Ericsson's most counterintuitive findings concerned feedback. Expert performers do not merely practice more — they practice with richer and more immediate feedback loops. A student playing piano alone can repeat a passage incorrectly for months without detecting the error. A master teacher hears it in seconds. This is why coaching accelerates development beyond what self-directed practice can achieve: it collapses the time between error and correction. In domains where feedback is naturally delayed — management, writing, research — practitioners must construct artificial feedback structures: submitting work for critique, reviewing recordings, tracking measurable outcomes. The principle is the same: without rapid, accurate feedback, effort does not translate into improvement.
question: According to the passage, why does coaching accelerate skill development beyond what self-directed practice achieves?

options.text: Coaches provide motivational accountability that keeps practitioners from quitting
Coaches design more systematic curricula than individuals can create for themselves
Coaching collapses the time between error and correction through immediate feedback
Coaches model expert performance that practitioners can directly imitate
